description:
GOAL: Land src/ir_inline.rs with the building blocks. No actual rewriting of
call sites yet — that's .15.2+. This ticket delivers primitives and tests
them in isolation.

Parent: fz-ul4.11.15.

## Deliverables

src/ir_inline.rs (new, ~200 LOC):

  // Constants — user-decided 2026-05-11.
  pub const INLINE_BUDGET: usize = 8;        // total Stmt::Let cap on callee
  pub const MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 3;
  pub const GROWTH_CAP: usize = 4;           // per-fn post-inline ≤ 4× orig

  /// True iff f has no Call/TailCall/CallClosure/TailCallClosure terminators.
  pub fn is_leaf(f: &FnIr) -> bool;

  /// Total Stmt::Let count across all blocks.
  pub fn stmt_count(f: &FnIr) -> usize;

  /// Convenience: is_leaf && stmt_count <= INLINE_BUDGET.
  pub fn is_inlinable(f: &FnIr) -> bool;

  /// Produce a deep clone of `callee` with all Var ids shifted past caller's
  /// current max Var and all BlockIds shifted past caller's current max
  /// BlockId. Returns the renumbered FnIr plus the maps for the splice step.
  ///
  /// Note: Cont.fn_id is NOT remapped (it points to a separate FnIr in the
  /// Module). FnId fields in Term::Call/TailCall (callee) and
  /// Prim::MakeClosure are also NOT remapped — they reference fns in the
  /// Module, not vars/blocks of the function being copied.
  pub fn alpha_rename(callee: &FnIr, caller: &FnIr)
      -> (FnIr, HashMap<Var, Var>, HashMap<BlockId, BlockId>);

  /// Append `renamed`'s blocks to caller; bump caller's internal block
  /// counter. Returns the renamed entry-block id (caller of this fn knows
  /// where to Goto).
  pub fn splice_blocks(caller: &mut FnIr, renamed: FnIr) -> BlockId;

NO call-site rewriting in this ticket. `inline_module` is not yet introduced.

## Var/Block max tracking

FnIr does not currently expose a `next_var`/`next_block` field — those live
in FnBuilder. The inliner must compute max-used Var/BlockId by walking the
fn. Add private helpers:

  fn max_var(f: &FnIr) -> u32;
  fn max_block(f: &FnIr) -> u32;

Both walk every Stmt/Term/Block exhaustively (every Var-bearing Prim variant,
including BitFieldIr/BitFieldPatIr nested vars; every Term variant's args/
captured/closure/condition).

## Exhaustive var-remap surface (do NOT silently skip any)

  Stmts:
    Let(Var, Prim) — remap LHS Var, then walk Prim:
      Const                  — no vars
      BinOp(_, a, b)         — remap a, b
      UnOp(_, a)             — remap a
      AllocStruct(_, vars)   — remap each
      Builtin(_, vars)       — remap each
      ListCons(h, t)         — remap h, t
      ListHead(l)/ListTail(l)/ListIsNil(l) — remap l
      MakeTuple(vars)        — remap each
      TupleField(v, _)       — remap v
      MakeList(vars, tail)   — remap each + Option<tail>
      MakeClosure(_, vars)   — remap captured vars (FnId is NOT remapped)
      MakeMap(pairs)         — remap k,v in each pair
      MapUpdate(base, pairs) — remap base + each pair
      MapGet(m, k)           — remap m, k
      MakeVec(_, vars)       — remap each
      MakeBitstring(fields)  — for each: remap value; remap size if Var(_)
      BitReaderInit(v)       — remap v
      BitReadField { reader, size, .. } — remap reader; remap size if Var(_)
      BitReaderDone(v)       — remap v

  Block.params: remap each.

  Terms:
    Goto(BlockId, args)              — remap block; remap each arg
    If(c, t, e)                      — remap c; remap block ids
    Call { callee, args, cont }      — remap each arg; remap cont.captured
                                       (callee FnId and cont.fn_id NOT remapped)
    TailCall { callee, args }        — remap each arg
    CallClosure { closure, args, cont } — remap closure; args; cont.captured
    TailCallClosure { closure, args }   — remap closure; args
    Return(v) / Halt(v)              — remap v

## Tests

  - is_leaf: hand-built FnIrs for each Term variant (Goto/If/Return/Halt
    return true; Call/TailCall/CallClosure/TailCallClosure return false).
  - stmt_count: 0-stmt, 1-stmt, 3-stmt-across-2-blocks.
  - is_inlinable: leaf+small=true; leaf+9stmts=false; non-leaf=false.
  - alpha_rename: var ranges don't overlap caller's; block ids don't overlap;
    one targeted test per Prim variant listed above to catch missed surfaces.
    Special: a MakeBitstring with BitSizeIr::Var(v); a BitReadField with
    BitSizeIr::Var(v).
  - splice_blocks: caller.blocks.len() grows by renamed.blocks.len();
    renamed entry's new id is returned correctly; caller's original blocks
    untouched.

## Acceptance

  - cargo test passes (no regressions in the 345-test suite).
  - New tests pass.
  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.15.1.

## References

  - src/fz_ir.rs:155-205 (Prim variants including BitFieldIr/BitFieldPatIr).
  - src/fz_ir.rs:220-246 (Term variants).
  - src/fz_ir.rs:213-218 (Cont).
title: IR inliner skeleton + leaf detector + alpha-rename
